title: QA run: browser dogfood 2026-05-13
description: Browser-driven QA run for Substrukt using agent-browser against a local throwaway data directory. Scope: first-run setup, apps dashboard, user invitation, signup URL/token flow, and obvious UI/UX issues found while using the product as a normal user.
close_reason: Completed browser QA loop for first-admin and invite-token auth flows; fixed the blocking invite and public-registration issues.

title: Build fails against local allowthem EmailSender API
description: Discovered while launching local server for browser QA.  fails in src/email.rs because local ../allowthem has changed EmailSender::send to accept &EmailMessage, and EmailMessage now exposes to/subject/template rather than body/html. Fix is required before product QA can continue.
close_reason: Fixed local allowthem EmailSender and lifecycle event API drift; cargo check --target-dir target passes.

title: Local server cannot listen under sandbox
description: During browser QA,  built successfully but exited with Operation not permitted at src/main.rs:392 while binding/listening on localhost. This is an environment/sandbox blocker; rerun server outside sandbox/escalated for QA.
close_reason: Environment-only blocker resolved by running target/debug/substrukt serve with escalation; server is listening on port 3157.

title: Public registration is available after setup
description: Browser QA shows /register remains publicly available after the first admin exists. /register?token=... uses the generic AllowThem registration form and created a user without consuming the pending invite or assigning/granting the invite's role/access. Need confirm /register without token is also public and gate registration behind a valid invitation or first-run no-users state. Evidence overlaps sk-xxn screenshots in docs/qa-browser-run-2026-05-13/.
close_reason: Fixed by disabling public registration unless --enable-registrations is set while preserving valid invitation-token registration.

title: Invite flow shows a signup URL that 404s
description: Browser-confirmed invite flow bug. Admin Users page displays a generated invite URL at /signup?token=... after POST /settings/users/invite. Opening that URL in a fresh invitee browser session returns Substrukt 404, screenshot: docs/qa-browser-run-2026-05-13/screenshots/invite-url-404.png. Trying /register?token=... instead shows a generic create-account form; submitting the invited email creates a user but does not consume the pending invitation or grant app access. Invitee lands at /apps with NO ACCESS, screenshot: docs/qa-browser-run-2026-05-13/screenshots/register-token-result.png. Admin Users still shows invitee@example.test as 1 pending invitation and the newly created invitee row shows an incorrect/default role control, screenshot: docs/qa-browser-run-2026-05-13/screenshots/users-after-token-register.png. Expected: invite link loads a token-aware signup page, validates targeted email, creates the user, assigns invited role, grants appropriate app access, consumes the invitation, and does not allow unrelated public registration.
close_reason: Fixed invite URLs to use /register?token=..., added shared AllowThem invitation-token registration handling, verified invite acceptance assigns role/access and consumes invite.
